gates.fail_current: PASS (normal default is impure: attention owned HIP; violations=['decode_attention'])
gates.pass_after: PASS (forcing generated attention makes all hot families pure)
gates.explicit_rollback: PASS (a named rollback flag surfaces a violation naming the route + scope)
gates.runtime_binding: PASS (guard fires at model init; fail-current raises, pass-candidate loads)

note: TG-P5 refuted the generated 8B attention on speed, so on a NORMAL fast run PURE_MACHINE_SEARCH_ONLY=1 correctly FAILS (attention owned HIP is external_handwritten). pass_candidate demonstrates the mode would pass if the generated attention were selected (at a speed cost).

rollback_named_violations.replacement_scope: hand-authored Q6_K coop/partial UOp templates. Byte-identical to the generated route (decode_q6k_coop_generated), retained as the rollback/oracle one flag away (DECODE_Q6K_GENERATED=0).

runtime_binding.model_init_guard: tinygrad/llm/model.py from_gguf calls assert_pure_machine_search when PURE_MACHINE_SEARCH_ONLY=1
runtime_binding.fail_current: PASS (Qwen3-0.6B load with PURE_MACHINE_SEARCH_ONLY=1 raised RuntimeError naming decode_attention_owned_two_kernel external_handwritten_kernel)
runtime_binding.pass_candidate: PASS (adding DECODE_FLASH_BLOCK_TILE_G5_8B=1 -> model loads: all hot families pure)

boltbeam: emit_seed_policy(purity_required=True) tags the policy; selected rows already carry provenance + rollback
